chunk_summary: Guidance on calculating energy and protein targets for catch-up growth in children with faltering growth, using age-based recommendations and measurable goals for nutritional rehabilitation.
learning_objectives: Understand how to calculate energy and protein targets for catch-up growth using age-based recommendations.
Learn to assess the need for catch-up growth based on weight-for-length, weight loss, or growth trajectory decline.
Identify measurable outcomes and timelines for evaluating the success of nutritional rehabilitation.
Recognize when to taper intensified nutrition and transition to maintenance.
key_facts: Baseline energy and protein needs should be estimated using age-based recommendations, such as ICMR-NIN guidance.
Catch-up energy targets can be calculated by multiplying age-adjusted energy requirements (kcal/kg/day) by the ratio of ideal body weight to current weight.
Protein targets can be calculated using the same method as energy targets.
Weight should stabilize and trend upward within 2 weeks, with a return to prior growth trajectory by 4 weeks.
Catch-up nutrition should be tapered once the child approaches their pre-faltering centile band or Z score.
reference_quote: A practical catch-up method from AAP guidance is to multiply the age-adjusted energy requirement (kcal/kg/day) by the ratio of ideal body weight for the child’s length/height divided by the child’s current weight.
document_key_topics: Energy and protein target calculations for catch-up growth
